proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, mean ~65) are associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher levels of the 3-level ordinal feature_057 (0/1/2) are associated with shorter pfs_months (monotonic decrease).
Patients with feature_051=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_051=0.
Patients with feature_038=1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_038=0.
Higher feature_092 (continuous, mean ~3.8) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher feature_099 (continuous, mean ~3.8) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho=0.785; OLS slope = +0.176 PFS-months per unit; mean PFS rises monotonically across deciles from 1.33 (lowest) to 7.45 (highest). Univariate r^2=0.587.
Mean PFS: level 0 = 5.27, level 1 = 4.05, level 2 = 2.91. ANOVA F=3528.9. Difference (level 2 vs 0) = -2.36 months.
Mean PFS: feature_051=1 -> 3.70, feature_051=0 -> 5.05. Difference = -1.35 months. Welch t=-68.5.
Mean PFS: feature_038=1 -> 4.97, feature_038=0 -> 4.00. Difference = +0.97 months.
OLS slope = +0.465 PFS-months per unit of feature_092 (univariate).
OLS slope = -0.073 PFS-months per unit of feature_099 (univariate).

proposed_hypotheses.text: All six top features (feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_038, feature_092, feature_099) retain their direction and significance after mutual adjustment in a single OLS model.
The negative effect of higher feature_057 levels on pfs_months is modified by feature_051 status (interaction).
The slope of feature_078 on pfs_months is attenuated at higher feature_057 levels (interaction).
The benefit of feature_038 on pfs_months is modified by feature_051 status (interaction).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_092 + feature_099', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Joint multivariable model R^2=0.848. Adjusted coefficients: feature_078 +0.176, feature_057[1] -1.20, feature_057[2] -2.34, feature_051 -1.36, feature_038 +0.94, feature_092 +0.47, feature_099 -0.076. All directions preserved; all p<10^-200.
Significant but small positive interaction: feature_057[1]:feature_051 = +0.054 (p=0.002), feature_057[2]:feature_051 = +0.096 (p=1e-4). PS penalty is slightly attenuated when feature_051=1.
feature_078:feature_057[1] = -0.0036 (p=5e-5); feature_078:feature_057[2] = -0.0168 (p=2e-42). Slope of feature_078 attenuated at higher PS levels, but small in magnitude.
feature_038:feature_051 = +0.027 (SE 0.020), p=0.186. NOT significant; feature_038 benefit does not differ by feature_051 status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across categories of feature_064 (race: white/hispanic/black/asian/other) marginally.
Mean pfs_months differs across categories of feature_018 (insurance: medicare/medicaid/private/uninsured) marginally.
After adjustment for the top-six clinical features, race (feature_064) is still associated with pfs_months.
After adjustment for the top-six clinical features, insurance (feature_018) is still associated with pfs_months.
The benefit of feature_038 differs across racial categories (interaction).
The benefit of feature_038 differs across insurance categories (interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by race: white 4.32, hispanic 4.31, asian 4.31, black 4.27, other 4.41. ANOVA F=0.87.
Mean PFS by insurance: private 4.33, medicare 4.31, medicaid 4.28, uninsured 4.25. ANOVA F=1.48.
Joint F-test on race after adjustment: F=0.57. All four contrast coefficients within +/-0.03 with p>0.35.
Joint F-test on insurance after adjustment: F=0.71. Largest contrast (uninsured) -0.032, p=0.16.
Joint F-test on feature_038 x race: F=1.93. Largest contrast (other) +0.12, p=0.15.
Joint F-test on feature_038 x insurance: F=1.76. Only contrast reaching p<0.05 was uninsured (-0.13, p=0.029); joint test borderline.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 30 continuous features not yet in the model, at least one has an independent (adjusted) association with pfs_months at Bonferroni significance.
feature_009 (heavy-tailed continuous, range 0.02-777) is independently associated with shorter pfs_months after adjustment.
feature_006 (continuous, mean ~224) is independently associated with shorter pfs_months after adjustment.

analyses.code: for col in continuous_features: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ top6 + col')
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted screen of 30 continuous features. Only feature_009 (coef=-0.0051, p=2.2e-93) and feature_006 (coef=-0.00041, p=8.3e-17) survive Bonferroni alpha=0.05/30=0.00167. All others have p>0.05.
feature_009 retains independent negative association: coef -0.0051 PFS-months per unit; sign matches expectation for adverse lab marker.
feature_006 retains small but highly significant negative coefficient -0.00041 per unit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the ~80 binary features not yet in the model, at least one has an independent association with pfs_months at Bonferroni-adjusted significance.
feature_013 is associated with shorter pfs_months after adjustment for the top six features and feature_009/feature_006.
feature_043 is associated with shorter pfs_months after adjustment.
feature_109 is associated with shorter pfs_months after adjustment.
feature_067 is associated with longer pfs_months after adjustment.

analyses.code: for col in binary_features: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ top8 + col')
analyses.result_summary: Bonferroni threshold (78 tests): 6.4e-4. Four features pass: feature_013 (coef=-0.328, p~0), feature_043 (-0.287, p=5.8e-263), feature_109 (-0.201, p=8.2e-26), feature_067 (+0.181, p=8.4e-15). No others below threshold.
feature_013 (prevalence 41.8%): -0.328 PFS-months adjusted; sign matches adverse-marker expectation.
feature_043 (prevalence 35.3%): -0.287 PFS-months adjusted.
feature_109 (prevalence 4.5%): -0.201 PFS-months adjusted.
feature_067 (prevalence 3.0%): +0.181 PFS-months adjusted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 modifies the effect of feature_038 on pfs_months: the benefit of feature_038 is smaller (or negative) when feature_013=1 than when feature_013=0.
feature_043 modifies the effect of feature_038 on pfs_months: the benefit of feature_038 is smaller (or negative) when feature_043=1 than when feature_043=0.
feature_013 and feature_043 have a positive (sub-additive) interaction on pfs_months.
feature_038's benefit is essentially confined to the double-negative (feature_013=0 AND feature_043=0) stratum.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_038:feature_013 interaction = -1.628 (SE 0.018). The +0.94 main effect is reversed in feature_013=1 patients to a near-zero / mildly negative net effect.
Adjusted feature_038:feature_043 interaction = -1.457 (SE 0.020). Same predictive pattern as feature_013.
feature_013:feature_043 interaction = +0.502 (SE 0.018), p=1e-175. The two adverse markers' negative effects are sub-additive (overlap), consistent with shared biology.

Stratified means: feature_013=0 & feature_043=0 -> feature_038 effect = +2.52 mo (p~0); feature_013=0 & feature_043=1 -> +0.07 mo (p=0.16, NS); feature_013=1 & feature_043=0 -> +0.06 mo (p=0.17, NS); feature_013=1 & feature_043=1 -> -0.04 mo (p=0.52, NS). Benefit confined to double-negative stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the biomarker-WT (feature_013=0 AND feature_043=0) stratum, feature_038 prevalence differs across racial categories (allocation/access).
Within the biomarker-WT stratum, the magnitude of the feature_038 PFS benefit differs across racial categories (effect modification).
Within the biomarker-WT stratum, feature_038 prevalence differs across insurance categories.
Within the biomarker-WT stratum, the magnitude of the feature_038 PFS benefit differs across insurance categories.

analyses.result_summary: Prevalence of feature_038 in WT stratum (n=18,853): white 19.9%, black 20.4%, hispanic 19.5%, asian 21.8%, other 19.0%. Differences trivially small (<3 percentage points).
Joint F-test for race x feature_038 interaction in WT stratum: F=1.155. Crude feature_038 effects by race ranged +2.41 (white) to +2.93 (hispanic); homogeneous within sampling variation.
Prevalence of feature_038 in WT stratum: medicaid 20.3%, medicare 19.6%, private 20.3%, uninsured 19.6%. Differences <1 percentage point.

Joint F-test: F=3.82, p=0.0095 (borderline). Driven by feature_018=uninsured contrast (-0.353, p=0.002 vs medicaid reference). Crude WT effects: medicaid +2.77, uninsured +2.50, medicare +2.58, private +2.37 — small absolute differences.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the biomarker-WT stratum, the magnitude of the feature_038 PFS benefit is similar across feature_057 (PS) levels (no effect modification).
Within the biomarker-WT stratum, the magnitude of the feature_038 PFS benefit is greater in feature_051=1 patients than in feature_051=0 patients.
Within the biomarker-WT stratum, the magnitude of the feature_038 PFS benefit increases with feature_078.

analyses.result_summary: Crude feature_038 effects by PS: PS=0 +2.45, PS=1 +2.53, PS=2 +2.48. Adjusted interaction coefficients: PS1 -0.098 (p=0.025), PS2 -0.129 (p=0.038). Statistically detectable but small attenuation at higher PS.
Adjusted feature_038:feature_051 = +0.155 (SE 0.040). Crude effects: feature_051=0 +2.41, feature_051=1 +2.60. Slightly larger benefit in feature_051=1, consistent with hypothesis.
Adjusted feature_038:feature_078 = +0.0059 per unit (SE 0.0020). Small positive interaction; benefit grows ~0.06 mo for each 10-unit increase in feature_078.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between feature_078 and pfs_months is nonlinear (quadratic component significant after adjustment).
Log(1+feature_009) is a better-fitting predictor of pfs_months than untransformed feature_009.
Log(1+feature_099) is a better-fitting predictor of pfs_months than untransformed feature_099.

analyses.result_summary: Quadratic term coef = +0.00066 (p=6.5e-113); cubic adds further small improvement (p=6e-26). AIC drops from 130353 (linear) to 129845 (quadratic). Modest convexity: slope of feature_078 increases at higher values.

analyses.code: compare AIC of feature_009 vs log1p(feature_009) in adjusted model
analyses.result_summary: Log-transform substantially better-fitting: AIC 130353 (linear) -> 129498 (log). log_f009 coef = -0.152 (p=2.4e-279).
log_f099 coef = -0.301 (p~0). When log_f099 replaces linear feature_099 in the integrated model, fit improves (AIC drops markedly when both transforms used together).

proposed_hypotheses.text: An integrated model with feature_078 (quadratic), feature_057, feature_051, feature_092, log_f099, log_f009, feature_006, feature_109, feature_067, feature_013, feature_043, and feature_038 with biomarker interactions explains > 89% of pfs_months variance.

After accounting for feature_038 x feature_013 and feature_038 x feature_043 interactions, feature_013 and feature_043 main effects on pfs_months collapse to near-zero, indicating these are predictive (not prognostic) biomarkers.
Within the biomarker double-positive (feature_013=1 AND feature_043=1) stratum, feature_038 has a net negative effect on pfs_months (apparent harm).

analyses.code: smf.ols(final_formula, data=df).fit(); compare to no-interaction model
analyses.result_summary: Final R^2 = 0.8954, AIC = 112,038. No-interaction comparator R^2 = 0.8609 (AIC 126,284). Biomarker interaction adds ~3.5 percentage points of R^2 and removes ~14,000 AIC points.

analyses.code: inspect coefs in final integrated model
analyses.result_summary: Without interaction: feature_013 main effect -0.328, feature_043 main effect -0.287. With interaction: feature_013 -0.029 (p=2e-4, marginal), feature_043 -0.0003 (p=0.96). Biomarkers' apparent prognostic effect was almost entirely confounding with the differential treatment effect.

analyses.code: linear combination from final model: b_main + b_38x13 + b_38x43
analyses.result_summary: Predicted feature_038 effect by stratum: WT (013=0,043=0) +2.14 mo; 013+ only +0.50; 043+ only +0.67; double-positive -0.96 mo. Negative direction in double-positive stratum is statistically supported by very tight SEs around the interaction terms (each ~10^-80 to 10^-100 significance).
